relationships.description: Wife and co-conspirator who drives and then suffers from the murder
King whom Macbeth serves and then murders
Fellow general and friend — whose prophecy threatens Macbeth
Noble who becomes the instrument of Macbeth's destruction
Trusts Macbeth as his loyal thane
Fellow general who chooses honor where Macbeth chooses ambition
The one man the prophecy cannot protect Macbeth against
Their prophecies are true but designed to destroy

places.name: The Battlefield (Opening)
places.description: The field of Macbeth's military triumph — where his valor earns Duncan's praise.

places.name: Inverness Castle
places.description: Macbeth's castle where Duncan is murdered — the crucible of ambition and guilt.

places.name: Dunsinane Castle
places.description: Macbeth's royal stronghold where he makes his last stand — the fortress of tyranny.

places.name: The Blasted Heath
places.description: The barren moorland where Macbeth meets the witches — the fork between honor and ambition.

objects.name: The Dagger
objects.description: Both the imagined floating dagger and the real murder weapon. Symbol of the fatal choice.
objects.significance: Externalizes Macbeth's conscience — 'Is this a dagger which I see before me?'
rules: The hallucinated dagger appears only to Macbeth
The real dagger is used to kill Duncan

objects.name: The Crown of Scotland
objects.description: The crown that Macbeth seizes and that is eventually taken from him.
objects.significance: Tracks the entire arc — desired, seized, worn, lost. Its passage marks the tragedy.
rules: Legitimate succession requires moral authority, not just physical possession

objects.name: The Witches' Prophecy
objects.description: Three predictions: Thane of Cawdor, King, and 'none of woman born shall harm Macbeth.'
objects.significance: Drives every major decision. True in letter but treacherous in spirit.
rules: Each prophecy comes true but not as Macbeth expects
The prophecy about Banquo's line threatens Macbeth's security

moments.label: Macbeth the Honored Warrior
moments.description: Macbeth returns from battle as a celebrated hero. Duncan names him Thane of Cawdor.

knows: he is a valued thane and warrior

moments.label: The Witches' Prophecy
moments.description: On the heath, the witches prophesy Macbeth will be king. The seed of ambition is planted.

transitions.detail: The witches prophesy he will be Thane of Cawdor and then King

transitions.description: Receives the prophecy that drives all subsequent action

moments.label: The Murder of Duncan
moments.description: Lady Macbeth persuades Macbeth. He murders King Duncan in his sleep at Inverness Castle.

transitions.description: Chooses to murder Duncan — the fatal, irreversible choice
Murdered in his sleep by his trusted thane
Crosses the moral threshold from honorable warrior to murderer

moments.label: King Macbeth
moments.description: Macbeth is crowned King of Scotland. The prophecy appears fulfilled.

moments.label: Banquo's Murder and the Ghost
moments.description: Macbeth has Banquo killed but Fleance escapes. Banquo's ghost haunts Macbeth at the feast, unraveling his composure.

transitions.description: Destroys his closest friendship through murder

transitions.detail: Loses his sanity and composure at the feast

moments.label: Lady Macbeth's Madness
moments.description: Lady Macbeth sleepwalks, obsessively washing imaginary blood. Macbeth realizes the crown has cost everything.

transitions.detail: Recognizes that ambition has destroyed everything he valued

transitions.description: Consumed by guilt, descends into madness
His true despair becomes visible — 'Tomorrow and tomorrow and tomorrow'

moments.label: The Battle of Dunsinane
moments.description: Macduff's army attacks. Macbeth learns the prophecy's loopholes. Macduff kills Macbeth in single combat.

transitions.description: The crown is stripped from the tyrant
Killed by Macduff — 'none of woman born' fulfilled through C-section

moments.label: Malcolm Crowned
moments.description: Malcolm, Duncan's son, takes the throne. Scotland's legitimate order is restored at terrible cost.

transitions.description: Justice is served; the tyrant's reign is over
